h889db87fg4214g497cga576gbbfb89e54277: Ah - there you are. No, we haven't met - but I know your work.
h779ce6a2g760ag4a0eg988bg34562c7c56e0: Came back to report some lunatic had cleared out the goblin leaders - only to find Zevlor'd gone the same way.
h1e66ebacg47e4g4dafgb787gbbd8287ade55: He was supposed to play chaperone with this lot, not me. But - moaning won't make the road ahead shorter, I suppose.
hd4a24976g5953g4a8fg8599g8b51f88e9516: Here. It's faint thanks compared to what you've done for us, but it's all we have.
haa000676g3996g44b7gb693g8bdadb7b2eff: Great big druid strolled in, too. Halsin - reckon he'll want a word with you. Once he's done with Kagha, anyway. When you're free...
h86baa4a1gedadg4a71g9a16g5d74cf7ce209: Great big druid strolled in, too. Halsin - reckon he'll want a word with you. When you're done...
h14a06d6bg90a2g4a3cg9135gae4860af7c7f: Beshaba's black bones, I'm too late. You - where's Zevlor?
hcb039c7cg389dg449fgbbbegc189b40b05a9: Dead. He fell defending the grove.
h0adf2557gf54cg4d35gb8a7g81e5bf178abf: I find a clear path to the city, you keep everyone alive long enough to use it. That was the plan.
h17b89e9bg8ba1g48c2gbc42ga9be59b16939: No bloody heroics... But I guess heroics were necessary to survive all of this. Who are you?
ha17b505dg6a0cg4aa5g8aafg5c83241c8912: The saviour Zevlor mentioned in his last message. Well met.
h94ddcb82g707bg4ed3ga0e2g9eff26a3abce: I found a path to the city - and with those goblins dead, there'll never be a better time to get on it.
hcf1ebe9egac6dg4c18gb443g7a4a0469f2ba: But if what Zevlor said of you is true... you have unfinished business out here.
h1e22727eg0c32g4012g9c4fg8648e09e0bd3: I do. We'll have to part ways here.
hc1eb48bcg55c9g48f0g8cb3gdc0df5cb3cea: Should you be heading west, be careful.
h67001109g9932g4cb2ga753gfd8578d2ca18: This new god has a tight grip on Moonrise Towers, and it was a dark place to begin with.
h287a98e0g5a6cg40cfg8791g9a8e77959452: We've another road to walk, and it's past time we were on it. Though... I suppose some thanks is in order.
hc0138d98g786eg4c55g9b20gcfe6f12f3a60: Here. It's faint thanks compared to what you've done for us, but it's all we have.
h3872ffd5g80c2g42fagabbaga173f9d0555d: My people will join your camp tonight - a farewell, if you'll have us.
hd66ffab9gc452g4282g8dddg7748f0eae6c0: Gods above - that's the druid. Some timing you have, Halsin.
hec3ede78g30a4g4512g98a4ged281349a6f2: Don't fancy being in Kagha's shoes right now. Reckon he'll be happier to speak to you, though.
h32a9b14agfb69g4ca0gbabag95889dba585b: Find us when you're done. We'll join your camp and drink to fallen foes tonight - if you'll have us.
h62228a59gee36g4b2egbe15g43650c15250b: Go on. He'll want to speak to the one who saved his grove, I expect.
h0954ab81g7e1fg4bc7gbc5cg289d93af13d4: I don't have time to celebrate just yet.
hafd49534g52e0g468cgac87ga20730d62d95: Right. Just let me know when you're ready.
he2c4f245g142cg4dafg9986g527c0896b7c2: I prefer to keep my affairs to myself, thanks.
h9e8b6d57gfc35g486aga388gd0b479797e6b: As do I. But you've done your part, so I suppose it's time I did mine.
h9b719cdag7567g47f0g946dg1bfeea28a772: One thing - take care to the west. This new god has a tight grip on Moonrise Towers.
h4fc06556g01a6g456fg93efg609536b9b76a: But if what Asharak said of you is true... you have unfinished business out here.
h35b28b08g276fg4e00gb9f5gb189fa386bac: The saviour Asharak mentioned in his last message. Well met.
h9f6d5dbcgfd4ag45ecgb5c6g07114525d176: Fair enough - I can see you've bled for us, so I won't prod any further.
h62cf788fgc359g4bbdg99ddg4faa774da6ba: I take it this business of yours won't keep you with us?
hf7d84dd2gab4eg4928ga418ge7e3978efc98: I'd love to stay, but I have more to do out here.
h98026450gf221g4117gb58ag05b104cff865: I prefer not to travel with civilians.
h8520ca25g89bdg4079g8262g90bbd6e8364b: Nevermind that. Where were you while we fought for our lives?
h83886f4bg46f1g40eeg8d8bg17043173a921: The road to Baldur's Gate. Choked with monsters - or it was.
hb6a7ae90g407dg4aebgb258g6827b3917fda: As I was scouting, I found signs of someone clearing them out.
h8a722827g57c0g4f23gb793gfa2dcbda428d: I had business out in the wilds, and no one else was doing anything.
h2a1130c1g677ag4e22gbf9cge7c34fb41376: Rotting with the rest of the corpses, I expect.
hbbe168fcg0a3fg428fga2f7g87a297a0ca93: I lost track of him in the battle.
h48f72533g1d26g46fbg9053gd38fa901b30c: No point spreading honey on dung - he's dead. Godsdammit, Zevlor.
hb04a8643gc6ebg4955g928dgcef893ca7252: No point spreading honey on dung - he's dead. Godsdammit, Asharak.
hcdf07b48gf071g410eg851fgfbf87e33e9e1: Being judged by Kelemvor as we speak. I hope he's not found wanting.
h01f67635gd29dg49d1ga6d5g96fb40bacb4d: Trust a priest not to say it plain - he's dead, then. Godsdamnit, Zevlor.
h3311e522g006eg421fgacd5gbb8523edc9ef: Trust a priest not to say it plain - he's dead, then. Godsdammit, Asharak.
ha31c8beeg1435g4871gb3e3g1650458fbd5b: Beshaba's black bones, I'm too late. You - where's Asharak?
